title: 10 best herbal creams for hormonal imbalance

remedy_properties: Estrogen-like properties: Dong Quai contains phytoestrogens, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to balance and regulate hormone levels, particularly during menopause.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in Dong Quai creams help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance and symptoms like hot flashes and mood swings.

Blood circulation-enhancing properties: Dong Quai is known to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which helps regulate hormone production and balance by increasing the flow of hormones throughout the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its rich composition of ferulic acid, vitamin B6, and isoflavones. These compounds help regulate estrogen levels, alleviate symptoms of PMS and menopause, and promote a healthy menstrual cycle. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Angelica sinensis soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and promoting emotional balance, which is essential for hormonal equilibrium. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support women's health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Angelica sinensis creams have anti-estrogenic properties, which help in regulating hormonal balance and alleviating symptoms associated with estrogen dominance.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids present in Angelica sinensis creams possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to hormonal imbalance.

Ligustilide: Ligustilide, a sesquiterpene, in Angelica sinensis creams has been shown to have a regulating effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, thus helping to balance hormonal secretion and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for creams addressing hormonal imbalance due to their rich content of ferulic acid and other compounds that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of PMS.
Leaves: Utilized in creams to address hormonal imbalance due to their high concentration of ferulic acid and other flavonoids that help balance estrogen levels and reduce symptoms of menopause.
Barks: Employed in creams to address hormonal imbalance due to their content of ferulic acid and other compounds that help regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms of PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 kg of dried roots of angelica sinensis in late autumn or early winter.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 60-80°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind 100g of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the powdered angelica sinensis with 50g of beeswax and 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously at 60-80°c for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Chamomile's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances by reducing the negative effects of cortisol and other hormones.

Hormone-Supporting Compounds: Chamomile contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to support hormone production and regulation, particularly for estrogen and progesterone, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS, menopause, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).

Calming and Relaxing Effects: The soothing and calming properties of chamomile cream can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked to hormonal imbalances. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, chamomile creams can help regulate the body's natural hormonal rhythms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the skin. The apigenin and luteolin present in chamomile have been shown to have a balancing effect on the body's hormone production. This herb has been traditionally used to support the endocrine system and promote hormonal balance, particularly in women experiencing menstrual irregularities and mood swings. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, chamomile creams can help alleviate hormonal imbalance symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-androgen, which may help reduce the effects of excess androgens on the body, potentially alleviating symptoms of hormonal imbalances such as acne and hirsutism.

Luteolin: As a flavonoid with estrogen-modulating properties, luteolin may help regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances such as mood swings, hot flashes, and menstrual irregularities.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hormonal imbalances and related symptoms such as anxiety and depression.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and hormone-balancing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their apigenin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote hormonal balance in the body.
Seeds: The seeds are utilized for their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and hormone-regulating effects that can help alleviate hormonal imbalance symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean container.
Combine the cooled infused liquid with 500 grams of distilled water and 200 grams of vegetable glycerin.
Add 100 grams of candelilla wax and 50 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean mold and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Oestrogenic activity: Hawthorn cream contains flavonoids that have oestrogenic effects, which can help balance hormone levels by stimulating the production of estrogen in the body.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by hormonal imbalance, promoting a more stable hormonal environment.

Vasodilatory and cardiovascular support: Hawthorn's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve cardiovascular function can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance such as hot flashes, mood swings, and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its unique ability to regulate and balance the body's endocrine system. The bioactive compounds present in hawthorn, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), have been shown to stimulate the pituitary gland, which in turn regulates the production of hormones. This helps to restore balance to the body's hormonal system, alleviating symptoms of hormonal imbalance, including mood swings, hot flashes, and irregular menstrual cycles.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Crataegus monogyna, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help regulate hormonal balance by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in hormone metabolism, thereby promoting a balance between estrogen and progesterone levels.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid in Crataegus monogyna may help alleviate hormonal imbalance by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hormonal imbalances.

Triterpenes and Sterols: Triterpenes and sterols, including ursolic acid and beta-sitosterol, in Crataegus monogyna may help regulate hormonal balance by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in hormone synthesis and degradation, thereby promoting a balance between various hormones, including cortisol, estrogen, and progesterone.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their estrogenic and anti-androgenic properties, which help balance hormonal levels.
Fruits: They are used due to their high content of polyphenols and other compounds that help regulate hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and other phytochemicals that help stabilize hormonal levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers on a sunny day in autumn.
Extract the active compounds from the harvested material using 500 milliliters of cold organic ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter the liquid using a coffee filter.
Mix the filtered extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes then pour it into jars and store in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances.

Bioavailability Enhancers: Turmeric creams often include bioavailability enhancers like black pepper or ginger, which increase the absorption of curcumin into the bloodstream, allowing it to reach target tissues and organs more effectively and interact with hormones.

Adaptogenic Effects: Turmeric has been shown to have adaptogenic properties, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and hormonal fluctuations by regulating cortisol levels and promoting balance in the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to regulate estrogen levels, reduce inflammation, and promote balance in the body's hormonal system. Additionally, turmeric's ability to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress may also contribute to its beneficial effects on hormonal balance, making it a popular natural remedy for addressing issues related to hormonal imbalances in both men and women.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a phenolic compound, helps with hormonal imbalance by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with hormonal imbalances and related symptoms such as PMS and menopause.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a derivative of curcumin, helps with hormonal imbalance by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the breakdown of hormones, thus maintaining hormonal balance and preventing conditions like polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC, another derivative of curcumin, helps with hormonal imbalance by modulating the activity of sex hormone-binding globulin (SHBG), a protein that regulates the availability of hormones in the body.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hormonal imbalances.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain bioactive compounds that help regulate hormonal balance and provide antioxidant properties.
Stems: Stems are used due to their rich content of phenolic compounds, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in addressing hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of curcuma longa root powder and mix it with 20 grams of beeswax pellets.
Combine the curcuma longa powder mixture with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes while stirring constantly with an electric whisk.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping it with a hand mixer.
Transfer the curcuma longa cream to a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has been shown to help regulate hormones by increasing the production of progesterone and decreasing the production of cortisol, leading to a more balanced hormonal environment.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in herbal licorice creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances by disrupting the body's natural hormonal rhythms.

Phytoestrogens: Licorice root contains phytoestrogens, plant-based compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. These phytoestrogens can help balance estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance, such as hot flashes and mood swings.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and promoting a balance in the body's hormonal system. The cream's ability to reduce cortisol levels, a hormone associated with stress, also helps to regulate other hormones, such as estrogen and progesterone, leading to improved hormonal balance and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin that helps to balance cortisol levels in the body, which can become imbalanced due to hormonal changes, leading to symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and mood swings.

Licoricidin: A flavonoid compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps to regulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, which is responsible for the body's hormonal balance, and can help to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance such as mood swings and hot flashes.

Isoliquiritigenin: A flavonoid compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance, and can also help to regulate the HPA axis and promote hormonal balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are widely used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to regulate cortisol levels and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have a positive effect on hormonal balance and are often used to create creams for this purpose.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycosides and other bioactive compounds that may help to regulate hormonal imbalances and are often used in creams for this purpose.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 2 liters of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain the herbal liquid.
Mix 200ml of the herbal liquid with 100g of emulsifying wax and 100g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20ml of distilled water and 10ml of glycerin to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St John's Wort creams contain adaptogens that help balance cortisol levels, reducing stress and anxiety caused by hormonal imbalances.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance symptoms such as mood swings and hot flashes.

Prolactin-regulating effects: St John's Wort extracts have been shown to regulate prolactin levels, a hormone involved in lactation and hormonal balance, helping alleviate symptoms of PMS, menopause, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its adaptogenic properties. The cream, rich in hypericin and hyperforin, interacts with the body's natural hormonal system. It enhances the production of neurotransmitters and hormones, promoting a balance in the endocrine system. As a result, the cream may alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalances, such as mood swings, irregular periods, and stress-related issues. By supporting the body's natural hormonal equilibrium, the cream may contribute to overall well-being and hormonal balance.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, which helps to balance serotonin levels and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalances such as mood swings and anxiety.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): Possess estrogen-modulating properties, which may help to regulate hormonal fluctuations and alleviate symptoms of menopause, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), and other hormonal disorders.

Naphthodianthrones (specifically Hypericin and Pseurotin): May have anti-androgenic effects, which can help to counteract excess androgen activity, a common issue in polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) and other hormonal disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to balance hormonal levels and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
Flowers: The flower extract is used in creams due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate hormonal balance.
Stems: The stem extract of Hypericum perforatum is used in creams for its ability to regulate hormonal imbalances by modulating the body's response to stress and promoting hormonal equilibrium.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 part of dried beeswax in a clean environment.
Combine 10 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 20 grams of base oil in a small saucepan.
Simmer the mixture at a low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the flowers into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture at a low heat until the beeswax melts.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid helps with hormonal imbalance by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have a balancing effect on hormones.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used to create creams for hormonal imbalance as they contain a rich source of menthone and menthol, which help to regulate hormonal imbalances.
Buds: The buds of Mentha x piperita are used in creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high content of menthol and other bioactive compounds that help to stabilize hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh mentha x piperita leaves when they are at their peak fragrance and flavor.
Extract the essential oil from the leaves using a steam distillation process for 3 hours.
Mix 10 grams of the extracted oil with 100 grams of a base ingredient such as beeswax and coconut oil.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the desired consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peony's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances such as acne, bloating, and joint pain.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in paeoniflorin, a compound that helps to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.
Rhyzomes: Contain paeonol, which is believed to have a balancing effect on the hormonal system and help to reduce symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and hormonal regulating properties, which help to alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Infuse 50g of the dried plant material in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 70c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 10g of vitamin e oil and mix well.
Combine the infused oil mixture with 100g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 40c and stir until the waxes are fully dissolved then pour into a container to set.

remedy_properties: Vasodilatory and antispasmodic effects: Damask rose creams can help relax blood vessels and reduce spasms, which can alleviate menstrual cramps, bloating, and other symptoms associated with PMS and hormonal imbalance.

Phenolic Compounds: Phenolic compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hormonal imbalances.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn when petals are fully open for best fragrance.
Steep 50g of rosa damascena flowers in 500ml of distilled water at 90°c for 5 minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 40°c for 10 minutes or until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Elder cream contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hormonal imbalances, such as PMS symptoms and menopausal hot flashes.

Saponins: Saponins in elder cream have estrogen-like properties, which can help regulate menstrual cycles, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and reduce the risk of hormone-dependent cancers by binding to estrogen receptors and blocking excessive estrogen activity.

Phenolic acids: Elder cream's phenolic acid content has been shown to have anti-proliferative and pro-apoptotic effects on cancer cells, which can help prevent or treat hormonal imbalances-related cancers like breast, prostate, and uterine cancer.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its rich content of bioflavonoids and isoflavones. remedy_constituents: Iridoids: These compounds, particularly acteoside and 6-hydroxyacteoside, have been found to possess estrogen-modulating properties, which can help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in Sambucus nigra, including rutin, quercetin, and kaempferol, has been shown to have antioxidant and estrogen-modulating effects, which may help mitigate hormonal imbalances and related symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 250g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency.
Infuse 100g of the harvested flowers and leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container to remove the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture until it thickens to a desired consistency for application to the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain phytoestrogens, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Estrogen modulating properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion cream have been shown to modulate estrogen levels by inhibiting or stimulating estrogen production, which can help to balance hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms of menopause, PMS, and endometriosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers measure out 100 grams.
Dry the herbs using shade or low-temperature drying method for 24 hours.
Combine dried herbs with 200 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax melt mixture at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove solids and transfer to glass jars sterilized beforehand preparation time 1 hour.
Store creams in airtight containers in refrigerator for up to 6 months shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with hormonal imbalance because it contains unique compounds that help regulate the body's endocrine system. Flavonoids: High levels of flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, in highbush cranberry creams may help regulate estrogen metabolism and reduce the risk of estrogen-related disorders like endometrial hyperplasia, which can be triggered by hormonal imbalances.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried american cranberries vaccinium macrocarpon at peak ripeness for optimal antioxidant properties.
Combine 100g of dried american cranberries with 50g of organic coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Infuse the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate gentle extraction of beneficial compounds.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids and retaining liquid extract.
Mix 50ml of the cranberry-infused coconut oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil in a heat-proof bowl, melting at low temperature to create a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain a unique blend of botanicals that work in harmony to regulate the body's endocrine system. Rhizomes: The rhizome of valerian is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance such as joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground valeriana officinalis powder with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30-45 minutes to infuse the valeriana officinalis extract into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with 2% beeswax to create a stable and spreadable cream that can be stored at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Prairie toothpick creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to regulate the body's endocrine system. The natural ingredients, such as chamomile and dandelion root, help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to hormonal imbalances. Additionally, the creams' ability to nourish and balance the skin's natural microbiome supports the body's natural detoxification processes, allowing hormones to function properly and maintaining overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Dong quai: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dong quai helps regulate menstrual cycles, alleviate PMS symptoms, and reduce hot flashes due to its ability to normalize estrogen levels.
Red clover: High in isoflavones, red clover has been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, reducing symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness.

Rhyzomes: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation associated with hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zanthoxylum americanum bark and dry it in the shade for at least seven days.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for thirty seconds.
Combine one part ground zanthoxylum americanum with three parts coconut oil and mix well for two minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at a low temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit for four hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into glass jars and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: South American yerba creams help with hormonal imbalance because they contain a unique blend of botanicals that work harmoniously to balance and regulate hormone production in the body. The creamy formula is rich in phytoestrogens, which mimic the effects of estrogen and help to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings. Additionally, yerba's adaptogenic properties help to calm the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate hormonal imbalances.

Polyphenols: The polyphenol content in yerba mate has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Caffeoylquinic acids: These compounds have been found to exhibit estrogen-like effects, which can help balance estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of menopause, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), and other hormonal disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation associated with hormonal imbalance.
Barks: Used to make creams that stabilize hormone production, promoting overall hormonal balance.
remedy_recipe: Collect and dry high-quality achyrocline satureioides herbs for at least two hours in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, measuring 10 grams per batch.
Combine the herbal powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax, heating the mixture to 60 degrees celsius for one hour.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for whipping, taking approximately three hours.
Whip the cooled mixture using a hand mixer or whisk until smooth and creamy, storing the finished cream in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain potent phytoestrogens that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. These natural compounds, particularly isoflavones, bind to estrogen receptors, regulating hormone production and promoting balance. Additionally, marshmallow creams often include other botanicals like chasteberry and black cohosh, which have been traditionally used to support menstrual health and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, such as PMS and menopause-related issues. By addressing hormonal imbalance, marshmallow creams can promote overall well-being and alleviate related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Marshmallow cream's emollient properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with hormonal imbalances such as acne, rosacea, and eczema.

Adaptogenic properties: The adaptogenic compounds in marshmallow root, such as mucilage, help to regulate hormone production and balance cortisol levels, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate hormonal imbalance symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to hormonal imbalances such as endometriosis, PCOS, and thyroid disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation associated with hormonal imbalances.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to regulate hormonal fluctuations and reduce symptoms of menopause.
Leaves: Used for their antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting balanced hormone levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix 50g of coconut oil with 20g of beeswax and heat them together for 30 minutes at 180°f.
Add 10g of the ground marshmallow root to the heated mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify within 2 hours.
Once solidified, add 10ml of witch hazel and stir gently before pouring the cream into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain compounds that have a profound effect on the endocrine system. Nettle's steroidal and flavonoid properties help to regulate hormone production, reducing inflammation and promoting balance in the body. This can be particularly beneficial for women experiencing menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as those with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). By reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal equilibrium, nettle creams can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance, promoting a more balanced and healthy body.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Stinging nettle creams contain flavonoids, which have estrogenic properties that can help regulate and balance hormone levels by inhibiting the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to hormonal imbalances.

Saponins: Saponins in stinging nettle creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by hormonal imbalance, promoting overall hormonal balance and well-being.

Amino Acids: Stinging nettle creams contain amino acids such as glutamic acid and aspartic acid, which can help support the body's natural detoxification processes and reduce estrogen dominance, a common condition associated with hormonal imbalance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help balance hormone levels and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Containing sesquiterpenes and other compounds, which have a sedative effect on the body and can help regulate hormone production.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality urtica dioica leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities for the desired amount of cream.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder, then sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove lumps.
Combine 20% of the herbal powder with 80% coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f to 190°f over 30-40 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into glass containers, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2-3 hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hormonal imbalance are topical treatments that help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance by delivering natural ingredients directly to the skin. These creams are formulated with herbs such as Cimicifuga racemosa, Vitex agnus-castus, Angelica sinensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Paeonia lactiflora, Avena sativa, Lavandula angustifolia, Trifolium pratense, Silybum marianum, and Dioscorea villosa, which have been traditionally used to regulate hormones. If left untreated, hormonal imbalance can lead to mood swings, weight gain, acne, and sleep disturbances, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hormonal imbalance that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile can help reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritation, and balance the body's natural response to skin conditions. Additionally, herbal creams may also be used to treat symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, using herbs like black cohosh and dong quai.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hormonal imbalance is a blend of maca root, wild yam, and chaste tree berry. Maca root balances estrogen levels, while wild yam helps regulate progesterone levels. Chaste tree berry, also known as vitex, supports the pituitary gland in releasing hormones in balance. When used together, these herbal creams can promote hormonal equilibrium, alleviate symptoms of PMS, and support overall reproductive health. They can be applied topically to affected areas or used internally as a supplement.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain the root of the black cohosh plant, which has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms associated with menopause and PMS. The creams work by modulating the body's hormonal response, helping to regulate estrogen levels and reduce hot flashes, mood swings, and other symptoms. This natural approach can provide relief for women experiencing hormonal fluctuations, promoting a sense of balance and well-being. The creams also support the body's natural detoxification processes.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: It helps balance hormonal levels by acting as an estrogen receptor modulator, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.
Cimicifugoside: This constituent is believed to have a positive effect on hormonal balance by influencing the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, thereby regulating hormone production.
Rotundifuran: It helps to alleviate hormonal imbalance by acting as a phytoestrogen, which can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, thereby reducing menopausal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cimicifuga racemosa are primarily used because they contain high concentrations of compounds like actein, which are believed to have hormone-balancing effects.
Roots: The roots also contain cimicifugic acids, which help to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.
Roots: The roots of Cimicifuga racemosa are used in creams for hormonal imbalance due to their content of lignans, which may help to modulate estrogen levels in the body.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 part dried cimicifuga racemosa root in 3 parts carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Melt 10g of beeswax and 10g of cocoa butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Mix the melted beeswax and cocoa butter with the infused cimicifuga racemosa oil in a 1:1 ratio.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain the extract of the chaste tree, which has been traditionally used to support menstrual health and balance hormones. The creams work by stimulating the pituitary gland, helping to regulate the body's hormonal production and flow. This can lead to improved symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance, such as irregular periods, PMS, and menopausal symptoms. By promoting hormonal balance, Vitex agnus-castus creams can also help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with hormonal imbalance by regulating the secretion of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), which play crucial roles in the menstrual cycle.
Phenolic acids: They help alleviate hormonal imbalance by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which in turn modulate the activity of hormones and promote balance in the body.

Flavonoids: These compounds contribute to the relief of hormonal imbalance by acting as estrogen modulators, helping to regulate the body's response to estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their rich content of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, which are believed to have a regulating effect on the hormonal system.
Seeds: Seeds are used as they contain a high concentration of berberine and other alkaloids, which are thought to have a balancing effect on hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which are believed to have a regulating effect on the hormonal system and promote overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers and leaves from a trusted source at the peak of their potency.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture is well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain bioactive compounds that regulate the body's hormonal balance. The cream's active ingredients, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the body, promoting a state of balance and equilibrium. This leads to a more stable hormonal system, alleviating symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance.
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: This phenolic compound helps with hormonal imbalance by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby regulating hormone production and balance.
Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-estrogenic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance such as hot flashes and mood swings.

Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help regulate hormone production and reduce symptoms of hormonal imbalance such as menstrual cramps and mood disturbances.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are the primary part used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high content of ferulic acid and other phytoestrogens that help regulate estrogen levels.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Angelica sinensis are used because they contain a rich source of danggui, a compound that helps to balance hormones and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.

Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high content of sesquiterpenes and other compounds that help to regulate hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried angelica sinensis root in the morning when the dew is present.
Steam 500 milliliters of distilled water for 10-15 minutes to create a herbal decoction.
Soak 20-30 grams of angelica sinensis root in the herbal decoction for 30-40 minutes.
Mix the infused angelica sinensis with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its adaptogenic properties, allowing it to regulate and balance the body's response to stress. The licorice root extract present in these creams has been shown to support adrenal function, promoting the production of cortisol, which is essential for regulating hormones. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe hormonal imbalances associated with conditions such as polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) and menopause, promoting overall hormonal balance and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help balance cortisol and aldosterone levels by inhibiting the breakdown of these hormones in the body, thus regulating hormonal balance.
Liquiritin: Acts as an anti-stress agent by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which helps maintain cortisol levels within a normal range and alleviate hormonal imbalance.
Glycyrrhizin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-glucocorticoid activities, which help reduce stress-induced cortisol release and alleviate hormonal imbalances in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their ability to regulate hormonal balance and reduce stress.
Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their content of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 250 ml of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat for 15 minutes with 10 grams of the powdered roots.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of cetyl alcohol in a double boiler to melt them at 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the cooled liquid from step 3 with 10 grams of the melted wax mixture and 5 grams of sweet almond oil and stir until well combined.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain the extract of Peony root, a natural herb known for its adaptogenic properties. It helps to regulate the body's response to stress, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance. The creams work by stimulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, promoting the production of hormones in a balanced manner. This results in improved menstrual cycles, reduced symptoms of menopause, and a decrease in stress-related hormonal fluctuations. It also supports the body's natural hormonal balance, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with hormonal imbalance by acting as antioxidants and modulating estrogen levels, thus reducing symptoms associated with hormonal fluctuations.
Saponins: Assist in regulating hormonal balance by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to stabilize and balance hormone production.
Coumarins: Contribute to the regulation of hormonal balance by exhibiting anti-estrogenic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, such as hot flashes and mood swings.

Flowers: Used to make creams for hormonal imbalance because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2 tablespoons of fresh paeonia lactiflora roots in 2 cups of distilled water for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to obtain 1 cup of herbal liquid extract in 30 minutes.
Mix the herbal liquid extract with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 180°f for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth and even consistency in 15 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden before whipping it into a light and fluffy cream in 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and regulate the endocrine system. The herb Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, has been traditionally used to support adrenal function and balance cortisol levels, which can become imbalanced due to stress, anxiety, or hormonal fluctuations. By reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance, Avena sativa creams can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, such as mood swings, fatigue, and insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Avenacoside: It has a balancing effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, which is often disrupted in hormonal imbalances, thereby helping to regulate hormonal production and secretion.

Stigmasterol: As a plant sterol, it has anti-androgenic properties, which can help counteract the effects of excess androgens (male hormones) on the body, thus promoting a better balance between androgens and estrogens (female hormones).

Beta-sitosterol: This phytosterol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, such as mood swings and hot flashes, by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The most commonly used part for hormonal balance creams is the seed, due to its high content of isocitric acid, which helps to regulate hormonal imbalances and improve reproductive health.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa contain a high amount of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and hormone-regulating properties, making them useful for creams targeting hormonal imbalance.
Leaves: Avena sativa leaves are also used in creams for hormonal balance due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and hormone-regulating effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean and dry the avena sativa roots to prevent contamination and preserve their potency.
Steep 1 cup of avena sativa roots in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then add 1/2 cup of beeswax to 1/2 cup of carrier oil.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with hormonal imbalance because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The calming effects of lavender oil can help to regulate the body's natural hormonal balance, particularly in women experiencing menstrual irregularities or menopausal symptoms. The essential oil's ability to lower cortisol levels and promote a sense of calm can also aid in alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, often associated with hormonal imbalances. This can lead to a more balanced and stable hormonal system.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with hormonal balance by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which in turn helps to regulate the body's hormonal response to stress.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a calming effect on the body, which helps to reduce stress-related hormonal imbalances and promote a sense of well-being.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Flowers: These are used in creams to make Lavender oil, which is commonly used to help regulate hormones due to its calming and balancing properties.
Leaves: These are used to create a tea that is often infused into creams, helping to balance hormonal imbalances and promote relaxation.
Roots: These are used in herbal remedies to treat hormonal issues, as they are believed to possess properties that help regulate the body's endocrine system.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong liquid.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the herbal liquid and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, which mimic estrogen in the body. These compounds can help alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings, by regulating estrogen levels. Additionally, the creams may support the health of the thyroid gland, which is often linked to hormonal balance. By promoting balance in estrogen and thyroid hormone levels, Trifolium pratense creams may help reduce symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance. This can lead to improved overall well-being and quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: They help with hormonal imbalance by acting as estrogen mimics, which can alleviate menopausal symptoms and support bone health.

Coumarins: They contribute to hormonal balance by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help regulate hormonal fluctuations and reduce the risk of hormone-related disorders.

Saponins: They aid in hormonal balance by modulating the activity of various enzymes involved in hormone production and metabolism, which can help regulate the body's hormonal response to stress and other factors.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their isoflavone content, which has estrogenic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Trifolium pratense are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high content of isoflavones, which can help alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Trifolium pratense are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their rich isoflavone content, which can help regulate estrogen levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh trifolium pratense flowers and leaves on a sunny day in july.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer for 7 to 10 days at 35°c.
Grind 200g of dried trifolium pratense material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 50g of the trifolium pratense powder with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60°c.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens and cools to form a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with hormonal imbalance because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant derived from the plant. This compound has been shown to support the liver's detoxification process, which plays a crucial role in regulating hormone balance. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting liver health, Silybum marianum creams may help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, such as mood swings, acne, and irregular periods. This natural approach may also support overall endocrine system function, promoting a balanced hormonal state.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Helps to regulate hormonal balance by protecting the liver from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can disrupt hormone production and balance.
Icariin: Acts as a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM), which helps to balance estrogen levels and prevent hormonal imbalances.

Flavonoids (including isoorientin and isovitexin): Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their high concentration of silymarin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-estrogenic and anti-androgenic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their ability to regulate estrogen and progesterone levels in the body.
Flowers: They are used to make creams for hormonal imbalance due to their potential to balance estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 silybum marianum flowers and leaves on a sunny day in late summer.
Clean the silybum marianum flowers and leaves in cold water for 10 minutes.
Combine 1 part silybum marianum with 2 parts vegetable oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and mix with 1 tablespoon beeswax to thicken.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dioscorea villosa creams help with hormonal imbalance because they contain the root of the wild yam plant, which is a natural source of diosgenin. Diosgenin is a plant-based steroid that can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. By regulating estrogen levels, Dioscorea villosa creams can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances such as mood swings, hot flashes, and irregular menstrual cycles. The creams also promote a sense of balance and well-being, making them a popular choice for women seeking natural relief from hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with hormonal imbalance by regulating estrogen levels and reducing the risk of estrogen-related disorders.
Steroids: Steroids help with hormonal imbalance by promoting the production of progesterone and other hormones, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of PMS.
Furostanol saponins: Furostanol saponins help with hormonal imbalance by inhibiting the conversion of testosterone to estrogen, which can help regulate androgen and estrogen levels in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dioscorea villosa roots are used due to their rich content of diosgenin, a plant-derived hormone that mimics estrogen in the body.
Seeds: Dioscorea villosa seeds are used due to their high concentration of phytoestrogens, which can help regulate hormonal balance in the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried dioscorea villosa root and 500ml of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the dried root in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 100ml of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken before transferring to a container.
